title: serratula cinerea
intro: Serratula cinerea, also known as White Saw-wort or White Serradella, is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Serratula cinerea include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and supporting the immune system. Serratula cinerea can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies for its potential anti-cancer and antiviral properties.
